component.name: DataTableToolbar
component.description: Responsive 3-tier toolbar for data tables with search, filter toggle, column visibility, and custom actions.

useCases: Search and filter controls above a data table
Hosting action buttons like Export or Import alongside table controls
Responsive toolbar that adapts from icon-only (mobile) to labeled buttons (desktop)

commonPatterns.name: full-toolbar
commonPatterns.description: Toolbar with search, filters, column visibility, and actions
commonPatterns.composition:
<DataTableToolbar
  table={table}
  searchValue={search}
  onSearchChange={setSearch}
  onSearchSubmit={handleSearch}
  isFilterOpen={filterOpen}
  onFilterToggle={() => setFilterOpen(!filterOpen)}
  activeFilterCount={activeCount}
  actions={<Button variant="outline" size="sm">Export</Button>}
/>

commonPatterns.name: search-only
commonPatterns.description: Minimal toolbar with just a search input

commonPatterns.name: with-filter-panel
commonPatterns.description: Paired with DataTableFilterPanel for full filtering experience

antiPatterns.scenario: Passing a table instance without any hideable columns
antiPatterns.reason: The View dropdown only renders when columns have getCanHide() returning true; passing a table with no hideable columns adds no value
antiPatterns.alternative: Omit the table prop if no columns need visibility toggling

antiPatterns.reason: Users expect Enter to trigger a search action; without onSearchSubmit the Enter key does nothing
antiPatterns.alternative: Always provide both onSearchChange and onSearchSubmit together

antiPatterns.scenario: Using the toolbar without a data table
antiPatterns.reason: The toolbar is designed specifically for data table patterns with TanStack Table integration
antiPatterns.alternative: Use standalone Input and Button components for non-table search/filter UIs

responsive.sm: Search on its own row; icon-only fluid-width buttons below
responsive.md: Single row with icon-only buttons and search input
responsive.lg: Single row with labeled buttons (Filters, View) and search input

slots.search: Built-in search input controlled via searchValue and onSearchChange
slots.filterButton: Built-in filter toggle button with active count badge, enabled via onFilterToggle
slots.viewDropdown: Built-in column visibility dropdown, enabled via table prop with hideable columns
slots.actions: ReactNode slot for custom action buttons rendered on the right side

interactions.search: Controlled input; calls onSearchSubmit on Enter keydown
interactions.filterToggle: Calls onFilterToggle; displays active filter count badge when activeFilterCount > 0
interactions.columnVisibility: Dropdown with checkbox items for each hideable column; toggles column visibility via TanStack Table API

responsive.mobile: Two-row layout: search row + fluid icon-only button row
responsive.tablet: Single row with icon-only buttons, tooltips on hover
responsive.desktop: Single row with labeled buttons and inline search

accessibility.role: Generic div container; child buttons and inputs have their own ARIA roles
accessibility.keyboardSupport: Tab navigates between search input and buttons; Enter triggers search submit; Space/Enter activates buttons
accessibility.screenReader: Icon-only buttons have tooltips that serve as accessible labels; dropdown labels visible
accessibility.focusManagement: No focus trap; standard tab order through controls

rules: The toolbar renders 3 breakpoint-specific layouts using CSS display utilities (sm:hidden, hidden sm:flex lg:hidden, hidden lg:flex)
Filter button only renders when onFilterToggle is provided
View dropdown only renders when the table has columns with getCanHide() === true
The actions slot renders identically across all breakpoints
Active filter count badge uses primary background with primary-foreground text
alternatives: Custom toolbar using Input and Button directly

aiHints.context: Use as the control bar above a data table. Pair with DataTableFilterPanel for the filter sidebar. Pass the TanStack Table instance for column visibility. The toolbar auto-adapts across 3 responsive tiers. Use countActiveFilters from data-table-filter-panel to compute the badge count.
